_publications._publicationTitle: Development of an adeno-associated virus vector for gene replacement therapy of NF1-related tumors.
_publications._context: Human MPNST cell line ST88-14, S462 and a patient-derived xenoline RHT92 were kindly provided by Melissa L. Fishel at the Indiana University School of Medicine | We maintained MPNST cell lines ST88-14, STS26T, S520, S462, 88-3, 90-8 and YST1 as described previously | The ST88-14 (RRID: CVCL_8916) and M724 (MPNST724, RRID: CVCL_AU20) human MPNST cell lines were gifted by Dr. Jonathan A. Fletcher | malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumour (MPNST) cell lines ST88-14, S462 and NF90-8 | ST8814, ST88-3 and 90-8 were generously provided by Dr. Nancy Ratner (Cincinnati Children's Hospital, OH) | Human MPNST cell lines ST88-14 and NF90-8 (generous gifts from T. Glover, University of Michigan, Ann Arbor, MI, U.S.A.) | Establishment of the YST-1 (Nagashima et al., 1990), STS-26T (Dahlberg et al., 1993) and ST88-14 (Ryan et al., 1994) human MPNST cell lines | Patient-derived neurofibroma (NF95.11b, NF95.6) or MPNST (SNF02.2, SNF94.3, SNF96.2, ST88-14) cell lines | NF90-8 and ST88-14 MPNST cell lines were generously donated by T. Glover (University of Michigan, Ann Arbor, MI) | Human MPNST cell lines S462, STS-26T, ST88-14, and 90-8 and an immortalized normal human Schwann cell line (iHSCλ2) were obtained from Nancy Ratner | MPNST cell lines STS26T, ST8814, ST88-3 S462, T265p21, S520, 90–8, and YST1 | Two million ST88‐14 (RRID: CVCL_8916) cells mixed 1 : 1 with Matrigel were injected subcutaneously into the right flank of male CD‐1 nude mice | ST88-14 (RRID: CVCL_8916; ref. 26), NF90-8 (RRID: CVCL_1B47; ref. 27), and NMS-2 (RRID: CVCL_4662; refs. 15, 28) | PDX cells and the MPNST cell line, ST88-14 (gift from Dr. Nancy Ratner, PhD, University of Cincinnati) | human MPNST cell lines (ST88-14, STS26T, S462, S462-TY, and T265) were cultured in complete media | The MPNST cell lines STS26T, T265, and ST8814 have been reported before and were gifted by Dr. Nancy Ratner | ST-8814 and S462 cells were obtained from Dr. Andrew Tee (Cardiff University) | Other MPNST cell lines and patient-derived xenografts (PDX) were kindly provided by Dr Nancy Ratner (Cincinnati Children's Hospital) (26T, S462TY, ST88-14, MPNST724) | ST88-14 is an established NF1-deficient MPNST cell line
